info.title: Generate generic JavaParser rule for AssertJ API breakage

parts.text: "Project @assertj-vavr/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @assertj-vavr/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/assertj-core-3.23.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-assertj-core-3.23.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC JavaParser transformation using the template in @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @assertj-vavr/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Considering code updates**

I see that I need to modify the code in the /workspace, but wait, is the repo even set up with git? So, I need to inspect the filesystem first. I think I should use commentary updates before making any substantial changes. It seems like I might have to create tasks, which is a bit complex. I’ll need to check the files and think about using multi-tool functionality to read parallel tasks as well. Let’s send a brief update.

I’m going to inspect the failing project, the dependency API docs, and the JavaParser template together so I can derive the generic transformation from the API change rather than patching this repo specifically.
